name: alert-dialog
description:
A headless controlled/uncontrolled alertdialog panel partial (role='alertdialog', WAI-ARIA APG Alertdialog + Modal Dialog). Renders INSIDE a consuming dialog WIRE component's owned template region; the parent's open @Wire field controls open-state. Provides a bounded confirm overlay: title (

, aria-labelledby) + description (

, aria-describedby, MANDATORY for alertdialog) + confirm/cancel action pair wired via l:click. Variant=destructive|warning|default drives the confirm button intent token pair + optional icon tint. Cancel button is always ghost + receives initial focus (least-destructive action, APG irreversible-step prescription). Focus trap, Tab/Shift+Tab cycling, Esc-to-cancel, body scroll lock, and focus restore are all owned by the shared focus-trap enhancer (data-lievit-focus-trap + data-lievit-escape-action on the panel root). No close-X, no scrim-click-to-close (data-closable='false' prevents it). confirmWireArgs + cancelWireArgs use the SAFE escaped channel (Escape.htmlAttribute per value). No @Wire field, no Java component. APG Alertdialog: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/alertdialog/
docs: Renders the alertdialog panel inside a parent dialog WIRE component's owned template. Pass title (required, wires aria-labelledby) and description (required, wires aria-describedby -- mandatory for role='alertdialog'). Wire the two action buttons via confirmWireClick + cancelWireClick (l:click action names) and confirmWireArgs + cancelWireArgs (SAFE per-row escaped args). Set variant='destructive' (default) | 'warning' | 'default' for the confirm button intent. Use loading=true to show a spinner on the confirm button while the round-trip is in-flight (cancel stays active). Pass iconSlot with @template.lievit.icon(...) for a variant-tinted icon left of the title. The focus-trap enhancer handles initial focus (cancel button via data-initial-focus), Tab/Shift+Tab cycle, Esc-to-cancel (via data-cancel-action), scroll lock, and focus restore. The parent dialog shell provides the scrim and overlay positioning.
